decisions.relationship_evidence: The study conducts laboratory syndromic surveillance for febrile or exanthematous illness and compares its arbovirus findings with traditional surveillance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application of laboratory syndromic surveillance to dengue, Zika, and chikungunya detection in Brazil.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Routine symptom data from telenursing calls were analyzed to nowcast COVID-19 hospitalizations and assess forecasting performance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete COVID-19 application of symptom-based surveillance data for local nowcasting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: PetBERT-ICD implements automated syndrome coding of veterinary health records and uses the coded records for earlier outbreak detection.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Provides and evaluates a computational implementation for automated syndromic coding and outbreak detection.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The perspective proposes a pilot sentinel scheme coupling syndromic and biological surveillance with sequencing for novel-pathogen detection.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Provides explicit programmatic guidance for strengthening and extending an African syndromic sentinel surveillance system.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The authors refine a respiratory-syndrome definition and develop a graded, multi-model surveillance early-warning framework.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development of syndrome definitions, anomaly-detection parameters, and graded early-warning rules.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study develops and validates an adaptive time-series method using emergency-department syndromic surveillance to nowcast severe outcomes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development and evaluation of a syndromic-surveillance nowcasting method using linked Australian records.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Gonorrhoea surveillance counts were divided by tourism exposure, producing destination-specific risks per million nights abroad.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete travel-volume-denominator risk estimation for travel-associated gonorrhoea in four Nordic countries.

decisions.relationship_evidence: European hepatitis A surveillance cases were combined with travel-denominator data to estimate risk per million nights abroad.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete travel-volume-denominator application for travel-associated hepatitis A across European countries.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Cohort-to-surveillance expansion factors were applied to national data to estimate underreported pediatric dengue incidence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Nicaraguan dengue application of expansion-factor incidence correction.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The modeling analysis adjusts recent HIV diagnoses for testing patterns and underreporting to estimate population incidence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete French HIV-incidence application of underreporting-adjusted extrapolation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Laboratory surveillance, outbreak investigations, and notification data were integrated, with survey-based adjustment for underreporting, to estimate enteric-fever incidence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete enteric-fever burden estimation in Guangxi using explicit underreporting adjustment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Hospital-record DALY estimates were recalculated under a Tanzania-specific 45% underreporting assumption.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete sleeping-sickness burden application reporting both observed and underreporting-adjusted DALYs.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Reported dengue cases, cost studies, and analyses of routine-surveillance underreporting were synthesized to estimate economic burden.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete regional dengue economic-burden estimate incorporating evidence on underreporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Laboratory-confirmed hospitalizations were extrapolated nationally and corrected for underreporting to estimate H1N1 cases, hospitalizations, and deaths.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete US pandemic-influenza burden application with explicit underreporting correction.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Nationwide surveillance was supplemented by independent case collection in sampled clinics to estimate underreporting and incidence without reporting bias.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete German intussusception-incidence application using an underreporting assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Registry and independent hospital-review data were combined in a maximum-likelihood estimator to correct substantial underreporting and estimate incidence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicitly describes a general maximum-likelihood approach for stable incidence estimates from incomplete surveillance data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Vaccination-associated surveillance was used to estimate case underreporting and improve morbidity estimates for an equine-influenza outbreak.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete outbreak application estimating underreporting and corrected morbidity.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A population survey estimated community gastroenteritis incidence and yielded 453 community cases for every nationally reported case.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Polish application reconstructing hidden community incidence relative to routine surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Literature and monitoring data were modeled to estimate marine-borne disease incidence and costs using explicit conservative assumptions about underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete US marine-pathogen burden and cost estimate accounting for incomplete reporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Representative serology estimated human exposure incidence over tenfold higher than reported clinical brucellosis incidence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Kyrgyzstan application using seroprevalence to reveal underreported human brucellosis burden.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Interval-censored regression corrected missed testing and diagnostic underreporting to estimate maternal toxoplasmosis incidence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Austrian application producing modeled incidence in place of severely underreported observed infections.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Dengue case reports were combined with case-underreporting and inapparent-infection estimates to calculate viremic-donation prevalence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Puerto Rican dengue application incorporating underascertainment into prevalence estimation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Administrative MRSA counts were adjusted using empirically estimated coding sensitivity to produce corrected hospitalization incidence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete US hospital application correcting administrative underreporting of MRSA infections.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Confirmed rotavirus hospitalizations were adjusted using underreporting measured through active gastroenteritis surveillance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete pediatric-hospital burden application with explicit rotavirus underreporting correction.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Surveillance-pyramid reconstruction models estimated pathogen-specific incidence while adjusting country-specific underdiagnosis and underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological reconstruction framework for converting surveillance counts into comparable community-incidence estimates.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Anti-tuberculosis drug consumption was used to estimate 80% underreporting and an incidence fivefold above notifications.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Greek tuberculosis application estimating corrected burden from an independent treatment proxy.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Reported dengue cases were adjusted with literature-derived expansion factors before disease and economic burden were calculated.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Southeast Asian dengue burden application with explicit underreporting adjustment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Empirical and extrapolated expansion factors were applied to surveillance counts to estimate regional dengue episodes and underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Systematic methodological analysis of expansion factors used to correct dengue surveillance burden estimates.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The Canadian foodborne-illness model explicitly accounts for underreporting and underdiagnosis and propagates uncertainty by Monte Carlo simulation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete national foodborne-illness burden application with ascertainment correction.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Multiple information sources were combined and extrapolated nationally, yielding listeriosis incidence above voluntary-system reports and demonstrating underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Spanish listeriosis application estimating burden beyond incomplete voluntary reporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A door-to-door prevalence survey was compared with hospital reports to estimate disease burden and reporting coverage.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Buruli-ulcer application quantifying hidden prevalence and the hospital-system underreporting ratio.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A negative-binomial time-series model used an enhanced-awareness period to estimate age- and sex-specific norovirus underreporting factors.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete German norovirus application estimating minimal reporting multipliers.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A population survey estimated gastroenteritis incidence, syndromic and laboratory underreporting, and annual economic burden.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Saint Lucia burden-of-illness application quantifying surveillance gaps.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Population and laboratory surveys estimated gastroenteritis incidence, reporting percentages, and cases per laboratory-confirmed report.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Grenada application estimating community burden hidden from surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The survey estimated annual gastroenteritis cases, 84% underreporting, a community-to-reported-case multiplier, and economic cost.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Trinidad and Tobago application estimating underascertained health and economic burden.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Population and laboratory surveys produced annual incidence, syndromic underreporting, a laboratory reporting factor, and total cost.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Dominica application estimating hidden gastroenteritis and foodborne-disease burden.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Survey-derived community cases were compared with syndromic and laboratory reports to estimate true burden, reporting multipliers, and cost.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Guyana application correcting gastroenteritis and foodborne-disease surveillance gaps.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A catalytic model inferred clinical brucellosis incidence from seroprevalence and quantified its excess over official reports.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Mongolian application producing an incidence estimate and underreporting factor from representative serology.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Alternative underreporting assumptions were used to re-estimate Ebola deaths and their resulting effect on life expectancy.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete West African Ebola burden application using ascertainment scenarios.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Canadian hospitalization and death estimates explicitly accounted for underreporting and underdiagnosis with uncertainty simulation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete national foodborne-disease severity-burden application with ascertainment correction.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Tick-borne encephalitis DALYs were calculated after applying corrections for underreporting and underascertainment.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Slovenian DALY application with explicit ascertainment corrections.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Dengue incidence modeled from official reports was adjusted with published expansion factors before global burden was calculated.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete global dengue-incidence and DALY application with explicit underreporting adjustment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Incidence from notification and surveillance systems was corrected for underascertainment and underreporting before DALYs were estimated.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete multi-disease Dutch burden application using corrected surveillance incidence.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Three claims-, proxy-, and laboratory-based projection methods estimated pertussis incidence far above diagnosed incidence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete US pertussis application estimating incidence hidden by underdiagnosis and underreporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Active vaccine-trial surveillance was compared with passive systems to calculate dengue expansion factors and quantify underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Southeast Asian dengue application deriving reporting multipliers from prospective surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Seroprevalence was converted to actual adult pertussis incidence and compared with reported incidence to quantify the hidden burden.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Czech pertussis application estimating incidence beyond routine reports.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A systematic review pooled serological data and used catalytic models to derive incidence unaffected by surveillance underreporting or underascertainment.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological synthesis of seroprevalence-based incidence estimation where routine surveillance is incomplete.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Probability-weighted outcomes among patients lost to follow-up corrected routine HIV-treatment mortality estimates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Zambian HIV application producing corrected mortality rates from sampled patient tracing.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Community and institutional active searches estimated attack rate and multiple components of chikungunya underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Colombian outbreak application quantifying cases absent from consultation and surveillance records.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Global youth tuberculosis notifications were adjusted for incomplete age disaggregation and underreporting to estimate incidence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete global tuberculosis-incidence application correcting incomplete notification data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Observed-versus-expected mortality yielded excess deaths, which were then corrected for estimated underreporting during the chikungunya epidemic.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Dominican chikungunya application estimating hidden mortality burden.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Observed mortality was compared with baseline expectations to estimate thousands of unreported deaths associated with the chikungunya epidemic.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Jamaican outbreak application using excess mortality to estimate an otherwise unrecognized death burden.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper adjusts measles-case completeness when estimating the conditional risk of SSPE, rather than estimating underascertained disease incidence or burden.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Its target is post-measles complication risk, a neighboring risk-estimation problem rather than this exact burden-estimation concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Adjusted case-fatality risks were used to infer country-specific detection rates and the number of undetected COVID-19 cases.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete multinational COVID-19 application estimating hidden infections from mortality data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Population and sentinel-hospital data were modeled to estimate pathogen multipliers and incidence beyond reported foodborne cases.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Shanghai application correcting underreported and underdiagnosed gastroenteritis incidence.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Serosurvey-derived underreporting multipliers were applied to reported infections to estimate total infections, hospitalizations, and deaths.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete US COVID-19 burden application using empirically derived reporting multipliers.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Delay-adjusted case-fatality ratios were used to estimate time-varying reported fractions and true infection levels across Indian states.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Indian COVID-19 application of mortality-based underreporting estimation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A serosurvey produced a case-to-undetected-infection ratio and infection-fatality rate for a South Indian slum.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete COVID-19 application quantifying infection burden hidden from reported cases.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Population-representative seroprevalence was estimated with bias adjustment and found to be sevenfold higher than official case statistics.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Orange County application estimating infection prevalence missed by case surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Representative seroprevalence was contrasted with registered PCR cases to estimate that only about one in four infections was reported.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Munich application estimating true infection prevalence and surveillance completeness.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study reports seroprevalence in persons seeking screening and only suggests that limited testing produced low case counts; it does not calculate a corrected incidence or burden.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is a serologic survey without an explicit ascertainment-correction estimate for the exact concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Serosurveys and a compartmental model were used to estimate unreported infections and deaths, corrected IFRs, and total epidemic burden.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Indian COVID-19 application quantifying infection and death underreporting across two waves.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper explicitly presents a simple mathematical model that converts age-specific reported prevalence into corrected prevalence and unreported-case estimates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development for early estimation of unregistered infections in epidemics.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A population-based serosurvey estimated adjusted prevalence, total exposure burden, and infection-fatality ratios despite weak surveillance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Nairobi COVID-19 application estimating infections not captured by routine case counts.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The authors develop and calibrate a natural-history model to estimate pediatric tuberculosis incidence and country-specific case-detection ratios.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit mathematical-model development for global incidence and underreporting estimation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Seroprevalence estimated actual infection prevalence and IFR and exposed a large discrepancy with reported cumulative COVID-19 cases.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Jakarta application estimating hidden infection burden from antibody data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Although prior-infection inputs were adjusted for underreporting, the paper's estimated outcome is presumed population immunity, not corrected disease incidence or burden.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The record addresses immunity estimation rather than serving as a lineage lead for this exact burden-estimation concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A transmission model fitted to serosurveys and death reports estimated cumulative infection and the proportion of COVID-19 deaths reported.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Delhi application reassessing infection and mortality burden under incomplete reporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study develops a novel ensemble approach that corrects reporting and serology biases and combines modeled detection, hospitalization, and fatality ratios.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development for bias-corrected daily and cumulative infection estimation across 190 locations.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A representative seroprevalence study estimates infection rate, a 2.68-fold underreporting rate, and infection-fatality rates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Saarland COVID-19 application estimating hidden infection burden from serology.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Serologic and postmortem surveillance were converted into multiplicative factors and corrected ranges of cumulative COVID-19 incidence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete multinational African application estimating true-to-reported infection ratios.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Underreporting and underdiagnosis factors were incorporated to estimate the true illnesses, hospitalizations, deaths, and economic burden averted.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Canadian Salmonella application correcting the observed reduction in reported cases.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Serologic antibody thresholds were converted into pertussis incidence estimates and used to demonstrate substantial underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Jiangsu pertussis application estimating hidden incidence before and after COVID-19.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Underascertainment assumptions enter the model, but the estimated target is the share immunologically naïve rather than corrected infection incidence or disease burden.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The developed method is for susceptibility and immunity estimation, not this exact burden-estimation concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Seroprevalence-derived pertussis infection rates were compared with reported incidence to quantify age-specific underreporting factors.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Italian pertussis application estimating infections missed by notification data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Published surveillance multipliers corrected flour-associated outbreak counts for underreporting and underdiagnosis before economic burden was modeled.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete US foodborne-illness application estimating hidden cases and associated economic losses.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The authors develop a Bayesian approach integrating serial serosurveys, registered cases, and antibody data to estimate time-varying underreporting and incidence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development for dynamic ascertainment correction during an epidemic.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study proposes fitting a power-law distribution to outbreak sizes to estimate missing small outbreaks and assess underdetection.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological use of a power law for hidden-outbreak estimation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Linked HIV surveillance data were back-calculated with missing-data imputation and reporting-delay adjustment to estimate incidence and undiagnosed infection.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Scottish HIV application estimating hidden prevalence and transmission incidence.
